purpose: Structured OpenAI-derived interaction lessons adopted selectively for local Mparanza plugins.

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI plugins ask when the answer changes source, method, destination, authority, or write scope.
patterns.local_rule: Do not ask the user to type continue for ceremony; ask only for external, destructive, approval-sensitive, or material choices.
patterns.playbook_section: Ask Only When The Answer Changes The Work

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI data workflows expose execution/source posture before rendering artifacts.
patterns.local_rule: State local data and deterministic-script ownership; record any external connector, upload, remote SQL, or hosted execution as explicitly approved posture.

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI widgets are action surfaces, not static summaries, when the user must review rows or findings.
patterns.local_rule: Review widgets must persist decisions to ui_decisions.json, apply decisions to applied_decisions.json, and update final_artifacts.json when decisions alter outputs.
patterns.playbook_section: Decision Capture Is The Interaction Contract
patterns.adoption_scope: plugins with row, finding, claim, prompt, or artifact review

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI plugin runs hand off visible app/card surfaces instead of expecting users to inspect hidden JSON.
patterns.local_rule: Normal review-session runs leave review_handoff.md or the workflow-specific artifact_card.md, and the card names payload, decision, apply, and final-artifact paths.

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI review widgets can be action surfaces that save the user's choices instead of becoming copy-only HTML.
patterns.local_rule: Generated local review workbenches must have a local browser or MCP bridge that persists save/apply decisions through the plugin's review tools.
patterns.playbook_section: Decision Capture Is The Interaction Contract

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI artifact renderers validate bounded snapshots before creating visible widgets.
patterns.local_rule: Validate review payloads before rendering; reject oversized or malformed payloads before returning a widget.
patterns.playbook_section: Validate Before Render Or Final Handoff

patterns.source_pattern: OpenAI plugins reuse host/widget conventions instead of creating unrelated interaction models for each workflow.
patterns.local_rule: Use the shared non-plotting workbench for comparable review workflows; allow custom stateful surfaces only when the domain needs a different interaction model.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: Remote warehouse execution as the default. Our default is local execution.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Local deterministic execution by default; explicit approval is required for remote SQL, hosted notebooks, connector reads, uploads, or external execution.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: Model-generated calculations where a deterministic script already owns the domain logic.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Deterministic scripts own mechanically verifiable calculations; Codex owns orchestration, review, narrative, and handoff.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: Asking the user to type `continue` after every progress checkpoint.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Ask only for material choices, approval-sensitive actions, external writes, destructive operations, or irreversible steps.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: A separate one-off HTML app per plugin.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Use the shared non-plotting workbench for comparable review workflows and allow custom stateful surfaces only for domain-specific interaction needs.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: Raw JSON review views when workflow-specific evidence panels are possible.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Render workflow-specific evidence views with bounded payloads, queue/detail panels, state filters, and artifact handoff instead of asking reviewers to inspect raw JSON.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: Static HTML pretending to persist decisions when it can only copy/download JSON.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Use MCP or the local review server bridge for durable Save/Apply; static HTML is only a fallback with explicit copy/download limitations.

rejected_patterns.playbook_text: UI polish that hides source gaps, blocked states, or missing evidence.
rejected_patterns.local_replacement: Show partial/blocked states, missing evidence, caveats, blockers, data posture, and final-artifact readiness as first-class review state.

playbook_section_coverage.section: Ask Only When The Answer Changes The Work
playbook_section_coverage.note: Interaction-level rule enforced across plugin skills.
Handled as run-intake and readiness evidence rather than a standalone interaction-pattern signal.
Handled through partial/blocked review states, blockers, caveats, and final-artifact validation.
Enforced through local/deterministic posture and execution-trace contracts rather than a separate UI pattern.
Interaction pattern checks that plugin skills declare local deterministic posture.
Handled by bounded render validation and malformed-payload rejection before visible widgets are returned.

playbook_section_coverage.section: Validate Before Render Or Final Handoff
playbook_section_coverage.note: Same pattern covers validation before visible review rendering and handoff validation.
Handled by review-state, blocker, caveat, and final-artifact contracts rather than a separate catalog signal.

playbook_section_coverage.section: Decision Capture Is The Interaction Contract
playbook_section_coverage.note: Catalog patterns cover MCP decision persistence plus local-browser save/apply write-back.
Catalog pattern checks handoff-card contracts for review-session workflows.
Handled by execution-trace, data-posture, and review-surface provenance contracts.
